summary: A glossy, vertical, music-scored recap of Secretary Noem's trip to El Salvador, Colombia, and Mexico, with no spoken transcript and kinetic on-screen captions throughout. It strings together a stylized shot of Noem descending airstairs, glamour-portrait pairings with President Sheinbaum, a roundtable B-roll about targeting Colombian drug cartels, and a closing slow-motion American flag waving over the slogan 'WE ARE PUTTING AMERICA FIRST.' The cinematic flag finale, music bed, polished color grade and slogan captions make it a produced America-First propaganda montage, not a raw event recording.

script.scene: Stylized shot of Noem (white top, ball cap, sunglasses) descending white airstairs into greenery; caption begins 'Secretary Noem reinfor...'
Posed portrait of Noem (light blue suit) standing beside Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum in an ornate wood-paneled room; lower-third caption: 'President of Mexico Claudia Sheinbaum.'

Roundtable with Noem and officials beside a U.S. flag and an 'Operation Return to Sender' poster (TdA arrests in Aurora, Colorado); caption: 'In Colombia, our partnership is targeting drug cartels to intercept drugs at the source before they reach America.'
Another smiling posed two-shot of Noem and Sheinbaum behind a vase of pink flowers in the wood-paneled hall.
Cinematic slow-motion close-up of an American flag waving against a sunset sky; large caption: 'WE ARE PUTTING AMERICA FIRST.'

notable_text: In Colombia, our partnership is targeting drug cartels to intercept drugs at the source before they reach America.
